block.create_radar.fire_controller.toolt: Emits a _Redstone Signal_ when the linked _Cannon Mount_ aligned

create_radar.data_link.select_mount_or_f: Select Cannon Mount or Network Controller

create_radar.ponder.controller_linking.h: Linking a Controller to a Monitor!
create_radar.ponder.controller_linking.t: Place Yaw Controller under turret mount
Select Target on monitor for turret to aim/fire at
create_radar.ponder.radar_contraption.te: Radar Dishes can be used interchangeably with Radar Plates
create_radar.ponder.radar_linking.header: Linking a Radar to a Monitor!
create_radar.ponder.radar_linking.text_4: Right click Radar Link to configure
create_radar.ponder.weapon_setup.text_1: Yaw Controller is placed under the turret mount
create_radar.ponder.weapon_setup.text_4: The fire control block emits redstone when the cannon is in the correct position
create_radar.ponder.weapon_setup.text_5: Place a Redstone Repeater on top to make the Fire Controller pulse. The repeater delay controls the firing interval
create_radar.ponder.weapon_setup.text_6: Connect to radar Network using Data links
create_radar.ponder.weapon_setup.text_7: Red Data Links indicate a Weapon Network, Blue Data Links indicate a Radar Network
create_radar.ponder.network_setup.text_1: The Network Controller is the core piece of the radar network
create_radar.ponder.network_setup.text_2: Filter items can be used along side the Network Controller to limit what it can see and do
create_radar.ponder.network_setup.text_3: Use Data Links to connect to a monitor
create_radar.ponder.network_setup.text_4: The same can be done for Radars
create_radar.ponder.tag.weapon_network.d: Components which allow the creation of Weapon Networks
create_radar.ponder.tag.radar_components: Components which allow the creation of Radar Tracking System
create_radar.target_filter.title: Targeting Filter
item.create_radar.binoculars.tooltip.sum: Allows for manual targeting of cannons connected to the paired _Network Controller_
item.create_radar.binoculars.tooltip.con: When Sneaking and right clicked on a Network Controller Block
When zoomed in with paired binoculars
item.create_radar.binoculars.tooltip.beh: Slaves any connected guns to the position you are looking at while _Zoomed in_ and _holding _L-Click_
item.create_radar.radar_filter_item.tool: Allows you to exclude certain categories of _Mobs_ and other _Entities_ from appearing on the _Monitor_. Filtered _Entities_ will also be excluded from autotargeting. Can be placed in the _Network Controller_
item.create_radar.target_filter_item.too: Allows you to enable _Autotargeting_, as well as set the targeting behavior. _Line of Sight_ will work outside of autotargeting. Can be placed in the _Network Controller_
create_radar.configuration.groundRadarCo: Color used for the ground radar on the monitor.
Size of each monitor grid box, in blocks.
Color used for hostile entities on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.friendlyColor: Color used for friendly entities on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.playerColor.t: Color used for players on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.projectileCol: Color used for projectiles on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.contraptionCo: Color used for contraptions on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.sableColor.to: Color used for Sable sub-levels on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.itemcolor.too: Color used for dropped items on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.neutralEntity: Color used for neutral entities on the monitor.
create_radar.configuration.monitorConfig: Client-side settings for monitor rendering and monitor interaction.
create_radar.configuration.disableMonito: When enabled, monitors always render blank. This can improve client performance.
create_radar.configuration.useGuiByDefau: When enabled, interacting with a monitor opens its GUI by default. Disable this to use the older interaction behavior.
create_radar.configuration.monitorLabelS: Scale of ship names and player names in the monitor GUI.
create_radar.configuration.radarLinkRang: Maximum distance, in blocks, between radar links.
create_radar.configuration.monitorMaxSiz: Maximum size of a monitor multiblock.
create_radar.configuration.radarGuidance: Turn rate used by radar guidance for CBCMW missiles.
create_radar.configuration.firingDelay.t: Delay used by lead calculation. Higher values can help on laggy servers.
create_radar.configuration.preventSableS: When enabled, Sable sub-levels hosting the radar or cannon cannot be selected as weapon targets.
create_radar.configuration.controllerPhy: Maximum rotational speed for physics bearings controlled by pitch and yaw controllers.
create_radar.configuration.binocularRang: Maximum range at which binoculars can acquire a target.
create_radar.configuration.radarStatsCon: Range, field of view, and speed settings for radar bearings.
create_radar.configuration.maxRadarRange: Maximum possible range of a radar contraption, in blocks.
create_radar.configuration.radarYScanRan: Maximum vertical scan range of a radar, in blocks. Targets above or below this distance are ignored.
create_radar.configuration.radarBaseRang: Base range of a radar receiver, in blocks.
create_radar.configuration.dishRangeIncr: Range increase provided by each radar dish or plate, in blocks.
create_radar.configuration.planeRadarRan: Range increase for plane radar, in blocks.
create_radar.configuration.gearRadarBear: When enabled, radar bearings rotate more slowly as more dishes are connected.
create_radar.configuration.radarFOV.tool: Field of view of a radar, in degrees.
create_radar.configuration.guidedFuzeCon: Settings for guided fuze target seeking.
create_radar.configuration.guidedFuzeMax: Size of the cone the guided fuze can track targets from. Values are in degrees and apply in both directions.
Maximum number of degrees the guided fuze can correct its course each tick.
create_radar.configuration.guidedFuzeSee: Determines whether the guided fuze can seek its target before it begins to fall.
